image.caption:
Fig. 4 Darriwilian and Sandbian conodonts. (a–c) Plectodina sp. cf. P.
florida Sweet, 1979; (a) Sc element, lateral view, WC3, JBNU25-25;
(b) Sb element, lateral view, WC3, JBNU25-26; (c) Sc element, lateral
view, WC3, JBNU25-29. (d, e) Plectodina onychodonta An and Xu
in An et al., 1983. (d) Sa element, posterior view, WC3, JBNU25-27;
(e) M element, lateral view, WC3, JBNU25-28. (f–i) Microcoelodus
asymmetricus (Branson & Mehl, 1933); (f) symmetrical S element,
posterolateral view, EC2, JBNU25-01; (g) symmetrical S element,
posterior view, YS2-13, JBNU25-02; (h) asymmetrical S element,
posterior view, EC2, JBNU25-05; (i) asymmetrical S element, poste-
rior view, EC2, JBNU25-06. (j–n) Tasmanognathus sishuiensis Zhang
in An et al., 1983; (j) Sa element, posterior view, EC2, JBNU25-07;
(k) Pa element, lateral view, EC2, JBNU25-08; (l) Pa element, lat-
eral view, YS2-13, JBNU25-09; (m) Sc element, lateral view, EC2,
JBNU25-10; (n) Pb element, lateral view, EC2, JBNU25-12. (o)
Erismodus typus Branson & Mehl, 1933; Pa element, posterior view,
EC2, JBNU25-13. (p, q) Tasmanognathus careyi Burrett, 1979; (p)
Sa element, posterior view, EC2, JBNU25-16; (q) Sb element, pos-
terior view, EC2, JBNU25-17. (r) Acontiodus viriosus Cui in An et
al., 1983; posterior view, EC2, JBNU25-31. (s, t) Panderodus gracilis
(Branson & Mehl, 1933); (s) P element, inner-lateral view, YS2-13,
JBNU25-32; (t) P element, inner-lateral view, EC2, JBNU25-33. (u,
v) Plectodina aculeata (Stauffer, 1935); (u) Pb element, lateral view,
EC2, JBNU25-21; (v) S element, posterior view, EC2, JBNU25-22.
(w) Protopanderodus nogamii (Lee, 1975); Sb element, lateral view,
WC3, JBNU25-35. (x) Erismodus quadridactylus (Stauffer, 1935); S
element, lateral view, EC2, JBNU25-24. Scale bars
=
100 μm (Park et al., 2025)

Fig. 5. A) Thin sections of the columnar to ex- tended bulbous reef frameworks used for con- stituent analysis (Table 1). B) Tracing of the reef frameworks made up of laminar reef elements such as stromatoporoids and bryozoans, enclosed by micritic sediments. (Hong et al., 2018)

Fig. 2. A) Outcrop of the uppermost stromatoporoid reef of the studied interval. B) Photograph of a set of thin sections showing domal stromatoporoid reef from the second reef horizon, overlying and overlain by peloidal–intraclastic packstone to grainstone (P/G). Note light-colored laminar stromatoporoids (arrows) bounded by darker micrite. The sample locations are marked in Fig. 1D. (Hong et al., 2017)
